scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: fetched Acuity's documentation index (developers.acuityscheduling.com/llms.txt) plus every individual reference page (each embeds a per-operation OpenAPI 3.1 fragment with exact request/response schemas) for every documented endpoint. Covered: 8 read streams (appointments, clients, appointment_types, calendars, forms plus 3 new: products, orders, labels) and 5 write actions (create_appointment, update_appointment, cancel_appointment, create_block, create_certificate). The write correction from the wave2 surface: cancellation is a PUT (not DELETE) to /appointments/{id}/cancel per Acuity's own reference page (put-appointments-id-cancel) -- the prior entry's method was wrong and is fixed here.

excluded.reason: single-appointment detail subsumed by the appointments list stream
reschedule is a distinct lifecycle action from update_appointment (date/time only, different validation path); deferred pending real usage demand beyond the covered create/update/cancel set
per-appointment payment-transaction sub-resource with no top-level list endpoint of its own; would require per-appointment fan_out this pass does not scope
creates a new staff calendar/seat on the account, which is a billable-seat administrative action beyond ordinary API-key scope
Acuity documents no GET /blocks list endpoint, only get-by-id; there is no catalog list to build a read stream from without a separate id source
removes a blocked-off calendar time range, potentially re-opening it to client bookings
computed availability slots for a given appointment type/date are a derived scheduling-UI query, not a syncable catalog object with a stable id
computed available-dates-in-a-month query; same derived-data reasoning as availability/times
computed class-availability query for group-class appointment types; same derived-data reasoning as availability/times
authenticated-account metadata (business name/timezone), not syncable catalog data
